_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-33R Methylprednisolone (MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-33R: Methylprednisolone — MDHHS Reviewed 2025
indications: Respiratory distress — severe asthma or COPD exacerbation
contraindications: Active systemic fungal infection (relative — discuss with online MC in emergencies)
precautions: Corticosteroids are ADJUNCT treatments — they do not have immediate bronchodilatory effects; onset is delayed (hours to days)
When oral prednisone is available and patient can swallow, consider oral prednisone (9-40R) as preferred for respiratory distress
Hyperglycemia may worsen in diabetic patients
Single prehospital doses are generally safe — multi-dose concerns are long-term
expected_effects: Delayed anti-inflammatory effect (hours to days for respiratory conditions)
Reduction in anaphylaxis recurrence and late-phase reactions (biphasic anaphylaxis)
Adrenal crisis: cortisol replacement — stabilization of hemodynamic status
notes: Adult and pediatric dose is the same threshold: 125 mg IV/IO/IM for adults; 2 mg/kg max 125 mg for pediatric
Prednisone PO (9-40R) is preferred when available for respiratory distress in patients who can take oral medication
Anaphylaxis: ADJUNCT — epinephrine is primary; do not delay epi to give steroids

dosing.notes: For anaphylaxis, adrenal crisis, or respiratory distress; PO prednisone preferred when patient can swallow
Same indications as adult; PO prednisone preferred when able to take oral
